title: gilia capitata subsp. pacifica
intro: Gilia capitata subsp. pacifica, also known as the Pacific gilia or sky pilot, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Gilia capitata subsp. pacifica include its potential to reduce fever, soothe respiratory issues, and ease digestive problems. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve making teas, infusions, or tinctures from its leaves and flowers, although more research is needed to confirm its full range of uses and effects.
